title: Airfield Checks
summary: The Checks module captures 8 types of ad-hoc airfield checks—FOD, RSC/RCR, BASH, IFE, ground emergency, heavy aircraft, construction, and other—with draft persistence across localStorage and the database, offline-queue submission, per-issue discrepancy logging, and automatic airfield status side-effects.
concept:
Airfield Checks are how AMOPS personnel document every unscheduled or event-driven check they perform on the movement area. Unlike the Daily Inspections (which follow a fixed section-by-section form every day), a Check is a quick, focused record: an Airfield Manager steps on the airfield for a specific purpose, documents what they found, and files the record. That record feeds the Events Log, the discrepancy queue, and—for RSC/RCR and BASH types—the live Airfield Status dashboard.

The 8 Check Types

All types are defined in CHECK_TYPE_CONFIG in lib/constants.ts:

KeyLabelNotes
fodFOD CheckDocuments a Foreign Object Debris scan of the movement area. No special fields.
rscRSC/RCR CheckRunway Surface Condition and optional Runway Condition Reading. On file, pushes rsc_condition and rcr_* columns to airfield_status.
ifeIn-Flight EmergencyCaptures aircraft type, callsign, emergency nature, checked actions, and notified agencies.
ground_emergencyGround EmergencySimilar to IFE but without callsign.
heavy_aircraftHeavy Aircraft CheckLogs heavy aircraft type for post-departure pavement check.
bashBASH CheckCaptures a Bird/Animal Strike Hazard condition code (LOW/MODERATE/SEVERE/PROHIBITED). On file, pushes bwc_value to airfield_status and logs a BWC change to wildlife history. Includes an inline wildlife sighting or strike form.
constructionConstruction Check3-section, 11-item FAA P/F/N/A checklist stored in data.construction_items.
otherOtherFree-form check requiring a Reason (captured at type selection, not after). The Reason appears in the Events Log "on the airfield" entry.

Check Lifecycle & started_at

When an Airfield Manager selects a check type from the tile grid, the following happens atomically in the click handler (lines 883–960 of checks/page.tsx):

  • A confirm dialog (or prompt for Other) is shown to make the Events Log consequence explicit.
  • checkStarted is set to true and checkStartedAt is recorded as new Date().toISOString(). This timestamp is carried through to started_at on the database row, providing an auditable "on airfield" time separate from completed_at.
  • A logActivity call writes the "AFLD3/OI ON THE AFLD FOR A <CHECK TYPE>" entry to the Events Log immediately, before the form is even filled in.

The type tile grid is disabled for other types while a check is in progress (disabled={checkStarted && !selected}), preventing mid-check type switching.

Draft Persistence — Dual-Layer (localStorage + DB)

Draft state is preserved at two layers so a check survives browser crashes, navigating away, and multi-device handoffs:

  • localStorage: saveCheckDraft() from lib/check-draft.ts serializes the full CheckDraft to airfield_check_draft_{baseId} on every state change. The beforeunload handler and component unmount both flush the latest snapshot.
  • Database: the manual "Save Draft" button calls saveCheckDraftToDb() from lib/supabase/checks.ts, which upserts a row in airfield_checks with status='draft' and a draft_data JSONB column. The row gets a DC-XXXX display_id.

On page load, a two-phase load runs: phase 1 reads localStorage synchronously; phase 2 fetches the DB draft. The newest-wins rule compares savedAt timestamps. If either source has a draft, a "Resume / Discard" banner is shown before the form is rendered. Resuming calls hydrateFormFromDraft() to restore all type-specific fields including constructionItems, issue list, and remarks.

Issues and Discrepancy Logging

The "Issue Found" toggle reveals a multi-issue panel. Each issue has a comment, optional GPS location (map pin or live geolocation), and per-issue photos. Each issue has a "Log as Discrepancy" toggle. On completion, the code iterates issues and calls createDiscrepancy() for every issue with log_as_discrepancy=true, uploading the issue photos to both the check and the new discrepancy record.

Offline Submission

Filing is routed through the offline write queue via getWriteQueue().enqueueOrExecute('check_file', payload). The check_file handler in lib/sync/handlers.ts calls createCheck() directly. If the device is offline the write queues to IndexedDB. The page listens for WRITE_COMMITTED_EVENT with detail.type === 'check_file' to refresh the recent checks list when a queued write drains.

Airfield Status Side-Effects

Inside createCheck() in lib/supabase/checks.ts (lines 126–163), two check types write back to the Airfield Status dashboard:

  • RSC/RCR: updates airfield_status.rsc_condition and rcr_touchdown/midpoint/rollout/condition. If rcr_reported is false, RCR columns are cleared so the dashboard shows RSC-only.
  • BASH: maps the 4-code condition to the normalized bwc_value (e.g., MODERATE → MOD), then calls logBwcChange() so BASH analytics can trend BWC history.

fits:
Events Log: every check writes two logActivity entries — 'started' on type selection ('AFLD3/OI ON THE AFLD FOR A FOD CHECK') and 'completed' on filing ('FOD CHECK CMPLT, NO NEW DISCREPANCIES'). These appear in the Daily Ops rollup and the Events Log page.

Discrepancies: per-issue 'Log as Discrepancy' calls createDiscrepancy() with the issue's title, location, and type. Photos are uploaded to both the check and the discrepancy via uploadDiscrepancyPhoto(). The linked discrepancy then enters the normal discrepancy workflow (submitted to AFM → awaiting CES action, etc.).

Airfield Status: RSC/RCR checks and BASH checks are the two live feeds that push condition data to the status dashboard without requiring a full Daily Inspection. This means a mid-shift RSC check immediately updates the dashboard's runway condition display.

Wildlife Module: BASH checks include an inline wildlife sighting or strike form (SightingForm / StrikeForm components) and write a logBwcChange() entry keyed to the check ID so BASH analytics can correlate condition changes with checks.

Offline Write Queue: the check_file handler in lib/sync/handlers.ts is the simplest queue handler — it simply calls createCheck(payload) and throws on error. Photos and discrepancy creates cannot be queued alongside the check (they need the row's ID), so the queue path shows a contextual toast naming what must be re-added after reconnecting.

PDF: a check PDF generator at lib/check-pdf.ts produces a printable record of the filed check, accessible from the check detail page at /checks/[id].

History: /checks/history shows all completed checks for the base, fetched via fetchChecks() which filters status='completed'. Draft rows (status='draft') do not appear in history.

quiz.q: When an Airfield Manager taps the 'FOD Check' tile on /checks, what happens before the form is visible?
choices: A draft row is immediately inserted into the airfield_checks table
started_at is captured and a 'AFLD3/OI ON THE AFLD FOR A FOD CHECK' activity entry is logged
The check is submitted to the offline queue and the form shows a loading spinner
The user is redirected to /checks/new with a pre-filled check_type
quiz.explain: The tile's onClick handler sets checkStarted=true, records checkStartedAt=new Date().toISOString(), and calls logActivity('started', 'airfield_check', ...) immediately. No DB row is inserted until the user either manually saves a draft or completes the check. The started_at value is later passed to createCheck() when filing.

quiz.q: A user starts a FOD Check, navigates away to /discrepancies without completing it, and returns to /checks the next day. What determines whether the localStorage or DB draft is loaded?
choices: The DB draft always wins because it is the authoritative source
The localStorage draft always wins because it is more recent
The draft with the more recent savedAt timestamp wins; if DB is newer it is used, but the local dbRowId is always preserved
Both drafts are merged field-by-field using the most recently updated value for each field
quiz.explain: The two-phase draft load in checks/page.tsx compares localDraft.savedAt vs dbRow.saved_at. If dbTime > localTime, the DB draft wins. Otherwise the local draft wins but receives the DB row's id (dbRowId) so a future Save Draft call updates the existing row rather than inserting a duplicate.

quiz.q: Which two check types trigger automatic writes back to the airfield_status table when filed?
choices: FOD Check and Construction Check
In-Flight Emergency and Ground Emergency
RSC/RCR Check and BASH Check
Heavy Aircraft Check and BASH Check
quiz.explain: Inside createCheck() (lib/supabase/checks.ts lines 126-163), check_type === 'rsc' updates rsc_condition and the rcr_* columns, while check_type === 'bash' maps the condition code (MODERATE→MOD) and calls updateAirfieldStatus({ bwc_value }) plus logBwcChange(). No other check types touch airfield_status.

title: Daily Inspections
summary: The Inspections module enforces one Daily Airfield Inspection and one Daily Lighting Inspection per calendar day (reset at 0600 local), with cross-user draft isolation, pass/fail/N/A item responses, linked lighting items that auto-create discrepancies and mark NAVAID features inoperative, and RSC/BWC/RCR values that push to the Airfield Status dashboard on filing.
concept:
Daily Inspections are the backbone of DAFMAN 13-204 Vol 2 compliance. Every operational day, an Airfield Manager must complete and file a Daily Airfield Inspection and a Daily Lighting Inspection. These are structured forms—not free-form notes—where every checklist item gets a Pass, Fail, or N/A response. Failed items with a 'Log as Discrepancy' toggle create linked discrepancy records automatically when the inspection is filed.

Two Forms, One Module

The Inspections page hosts both forms under a single route (/inspections). The form type is 'airfield' | 'lighting' and drives which section list is loaded (AIRFIELD_INSPECTION_SECTIONS or LIGHTING_INSPECTION_SECTIONS from lib/constants.ts, or base-specific DB templates). The airfield form has 9 sections (44 items including BWC, RSC, and RCR). The lighting form has 5 sections (34 items including RSC and RCR).

One-Per-Day Hard Lock

Before any new inspection is created, handleBeginNew() checks whether todayAirfield or todayLighting is already non-null. If so, it sets blockedInfo with the reason ('completed' or 'in_progress') and the inspector's name. The UI then shows a block dialog instead of starting a new form. The block has two states:

  • Completed: the day's inspection is already filed. The Airfield Manager can view it but cannot create another.
  • In-progress by another user: another Airfield Manager has the form open (todayAirfieldByOther = todayAirfieldInProgress && inspector_id !== currentUserId). The current user sees who has it and can choose to resume (takes over) or view it.

0600L Day Boundary

The todayStr computed value (line 1932 of inspections/page.tsx) determines which date is "today" for the one-per-day lock:

  • The installation's configured timezone is read from currentInstallation.timezone.
  • If the local time is before 0600, the previous calendar date is used. This aligns with the airfield operational day — a lighting inspection done at 0430 belongs to the prior day's record.
  • The resulting YYYY-MM-DD string is stored in inspections.inspection_date on every save and file operation.

Cross-User Draft Isolation

When the page loads, it fetches all in_progress inspections for the base from Supabase. The sync loop (line 371) skips any row where dbRow.inspector_id !== currentUserId. This prevents an Airfield Manager from silently overwriting a colleague's in-progress draft when they open the inspections page on a shared device. The blocked-info UI surfaces the other inspector's name so the situation is visible rather than silent.

Draft Persistence — localStorage + DB

Each form type has its own draft: glidepath_inspection_draft_airfield_{baseId} and glidepath_inspection_draft_lighting_{baseId} in localStorage, managed by lib/inspection-draft.ts. The SingleInspectionDraft shape wraps an InspectionHalfDraft which holds all responses, comments, discrepancy entries, uploaded photo IDs, BWC/RSC/RCR values, and the DB row ID. Saving to the DB uses saveInspectionDraft() from lib/supabase/inspections.ts. On Begin, a client-minted UUID (crypto.randomUUID()) is assigned as the row ID immediately; the initial INSERT is queued through the offline write queue so the ID is stable regardless of network state. All subsequent auto-saves (UPDATEs) and the final file write reference this same UUID.

Pause and Resume

lib/inspection-pause.ts provides a simple localStorage flag (glidepath_inspection_paused_{type}_{installationId}). When an Airfield Manager pauses, the flag is set and a "AFLD3/OI OFF AIRFIELD — paused" activity entry is logged. When they resume, consumePauseFlag() reads-and-clears the flag; if it was set, a "AFLD3/OI ON AIRFIELD — Resuming" entry is logged. The flag is consumed exactly once, so re-opening the page without a pause does not double-log.

Filing Side-Effects

When an inspection is filed (handleComplete()), several side-effects fire in sequence:

  1. BWC / RSC / RCR pushed to airfield_status: updateAirfieldStatus() is called with the inspection's final values. RCR is cleared if only RSC was reported.
  2. Discrepancies created: for every item where d.log_as_discrepancy === true, createDiscrepancy() is called. A client-allocated UUID is assigned up front so downstream writes can FK against it whether the create committed or queued.
  3. Linked NAVAID features marked inoperative: if a discrepancy has linked_feature_ids (set when the Airfield Manager picks a specific lighting fixture from the infrastructure picker), updateFeatureStatus(fid, 'inoperative') is called. An outage_event is also created, kicking off the DAFMAN A3.1 threshold analysis.
  4. DAFMAN threshold check: after all features are marked inop, calculateAllSystemHealth() runs and surfaces any systems in yellow/red/black alert tier as a toast.
  5. Activity log: a 'completed' entry is logged with the full condition string, e.g. 'AFLD3/OI off the airfield, Daily Airfield Inspection Complete, RSC/DRY & BWC/LOW, NO NEW DISCREPANCIES'.

Item Response Model

Each item uses a three-state toggle from PfnToggle — pass (P), fail (F), N/A. Items default to pass so Airfield Managers only need to act on items that are not passing. Special item types override this:

  • type: 'bwc' — backed by half.bwcValue, not responses. Pass = value is set.
  • type: 'rsc' — backed by half.rscCondition.
  • type: 'rcr' — pass if RCR is reported and value is set, or if RSC is set and RCR is not reported (RSC-only mode).

Newest-wins for same-user DB vs localStorage: DB is only preferred when it has strictly more responses than localStorage

code.caption: handleComplete: BWC/RSC/RCR pushed to airfield_status, discrepancies created, NAVAID features marked inoperative

annotations.note: statusBatch is assembled from completedHalf.bwcValue / rscCondition / rcrValue, then fired as a single updateAirfieldStatus() call

Discrepancy loop: each item with log_as_discrepancy=true gets a pre-allocated UUID (discId = crypto.randomUUID()), then enqueueOrExecute('discrepancy_create', ...). Linked NAVAID features are marked inoperative inline on the committed path.

code.caption: Auto-mark features inoperative and check DAFMAN A3.1 thresholds on file

annotations.note: bulkUpdateStatus(uniqueIds, 'inoperative') marks all linked features in one call; then createOutageEvent() is called per feature to start the NAVAID outage timeline
calculateAllSystemHealth() + getAlertTier() runs the DAFMAN A3.1 engine; yellow/red/black systems surface as a toast description with duration 8000ms
InspectionHalfDraft: responses keyed by item ID, separate bwcValue/rscCondition/rcrValue fields, discrepancies Record<itemId, SimpleDiscrepancy[]>, uploadedPhotos for already-synced photo IDs
normalizeHalfDraft: the single backward-compat boundary for both localStorage and DB blobs — missing fields fall back to empty-draft defaults, preventing blank renders from stale drafts

halfDraftToItems: converts draft state to InspectionItem[] for filing. BWC/RSC/RCR items are built from their dedicated fields, not from responses[]. Unset responses default to 'pass' (items default to passing).

code.caption: Pause flag — one-shot localStorage signal for resume activity logging

annotations.note: consumePauseFlag: reads the key, clears it, returns true once. Callers only log a resume activity entry when this returns true — prevents double-logging on page re-opens that are not resumes.

code.caption: AIRFIELD_INSPECTION_SECTIONS — 9 sections, 44 items including BWC, RSC, RCR special types

annotations.note: BWC item has type: 'bwc' — this drives the PfnToggle to show a BWC value picker instead of P/F/N/A. Same pattern for type: 'rsc' and type: 'rcr'.
Sections 8 and 9 have conditional: 'Construction Meeting Inspection' / 'Joint Monthly Airfield Inspection' — these sections only render when enabledConditionals[section.id] is true

fits:
Airfield Status dashboard: filing either inspection type pushes BWC, RSC, and RCR values to airfield_status via updateAirfieldStatus(). The dashboard's condition display reads these columns; an inspection is the primary source of truth for daytime conditions (BASH checks are the secondary source for mid-shift BWC updates).

Discrepancies module: inspection items with 'Log as Discrepancy' toggled automatically create discrepancy records on filing. The discrepancy's infrastructure_feature_id is set when a lighting fixture was picked from the item's linked-feature picker, enabling the Visual NAVAIDs module to show a discrepancy badge on that feature.

Visual NAVAIDs / Infrastructure module: linked_feature_ids on a discrepancy trigger updateFeatureStatus(fid, 'inoperative') and createOutageEvent() on the inspection file path. This is the primary automated path for marking a NAVAID inoperative and starting the outage clock tracked against DAFMAN A3.1 thresholds.

Daily Reviews: the inspections.inspection_date value ties inspection records to the Daily Review slot for that date. The Daily Reviews page (/daily-reviews) groups inspection completions alongside shift sign-offs for the shift certification workflow.

Events Log: three logActivity entries are created per inspection: 'started' (on begin), optionally 'noted' (on resume from pause), and 'completed' (on file). The completed entry includes the full condition string and discrepancy summary in AFLD3 format.

Offline Write Queue: the Begin flow immediately enqueues an inspection_save_draft write with the pre-allocated UUID. The file step enqueues inspection_file. Both use optimisticEntityId set to the same UUID, so FK relationships from discrepancy creates and outage events remain valid whether the writes committed inline or are draining later.

Construction Meeting and Joint Monthly forms: /inspections/construction/new and /inspections/joint-monthly/new create inspection rows with inspection_type: 'construction_meeting' or 'joint_monthly' and display_id prefixes CM/JM. They share the same createInspection() call path but do not enforce the one-per-day lock.

quiz.q: An Airfield Manager starts a Daily Airfield Inspection at 0415 local on Tuesday. What inspection_date is stamped on the row?
choices: Tuesday's date, because that is the calendar date when the inspection was started
Monday's date, because 0415 is before the 0600 local day boundary
The date of the previous Sunday, because USAF weeks start Sunday
The UTC date, regardless of local time
quiz.explain: The todayStr useMemo in inspections/page.tsx converts to the installation's timezone, then checks if localNow.getHours() < 6. At 0415, hours=4 < 6, so the date is rolled back by one day. An inspection started before 0600 local is stamped with the prior calendar date.

quiz.q: An Airfield Manager opens /inspections and sees a Daily Airfield Inspection row with status 'in_progress' started by a different colleague. What does the code do with that row?
choices: It loads the row into the current user's airfield draft so they can continue it
It skips the row (continues === true) — the current user's draft state is unaffected
It deletes the row as an orphan because no localStorage draft matches it
It shows a merge conflict dialog asking which draft to keep
quiz.explain: Line 372 of inspections/page.tsx: 'if (dbRow.inspector_id && dbRow.inspector_id !== currentUserId) continue'. Other users' in-progress rows are skipped entirely during the Supabase draft sync. The blocked-info state surface (todayAirfieldByOther) is computed separately from liveInspections and drives the UI block dialog.

quiz.q: When a lighting inspection is filed with a 'Failed' item linked to infrastructure feature ID 'feat-123', what sequence of writes fires?
choices: Only a discrepancy is created; feature status updates are manual via /infrastructure
createDiscrepancy() → updateFeatureStatus('feat-123', 'inoperative') → createOutageEvent() → DAFMAN threshold check
createOutageEvent() fires first, then createDiscrepancy() once the outage is confirmed
The discrepancy and outage event are batched into a single Supabase RPC call
quiz.explain: In handleComplete() the sequence is: (1) createDiscrepancy() via enqueueOrExecute('discrepancy_create', ...) with discId pre-allocated; (2) if hasLinkedFeatures and the create committed, updateFeatureStatus(fid, 'inoperative') inline; (3) after all discrepancies, bulkUpdateStatus() + createOutageEvent() per feature; (4) calculateAllSystemHealth() for DAFMAN A3.1 alert check.
